meta.title: AI Spend Review
meta.prepared_by: AI Spend CFO, by Aperio Finance
meta.generated_note: Precomputed once from the Northstar sample dataset and cached. No model call is made on the sample path (D3). The numbers are produced by the deterministic metrics layer; the language is the AI's.
meta.scope_note: This first review establishes the spend baseline and the control gaps. Budget variance and forecast scenarios are added once budgets are set.

blocks.text: Northstar AI Labs spent $6,260 on AI usage in May 2026 across Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google, with no formal budget framework or approval gate in place. The spend is concentrated and frontier-heavy: 79% ($4,958) ran on frontier-tier models, and the two largest line items, both in Data Science, account for 39% ($2,430) of the total.

Three control gaps stand out. Low-value workflows consumed $2,214 (35% of spend), almost all of it ($2,145) on frontier-tier models where a cheaper tier would serve. Unapproved usage reached $1,594 (25%), and $1,398 (22%) of spend carried no project owner. Finance, by contrast, was disciplined: 2.4% of spend, almost entirely high-value board and variance work. The picture is not a budget overrun, it is an absence of governance, and that is what the recommendations below address.

Spend ranked by workflow. Data Science model evaluation and synthetic-data generation lead, followed by Engineering's code assistant. Marketing campaign content generation is the fourth-largest line item and runs predominantly on frontier-tier models for low-complexity content.

Each flag below is quantified in dollars from the data. The flags overlap (a single workflow can be low-value, unapproved, and ownerless at once), so they are exposure measures, not a sum to be added.

items.label: Frontier models on low-value work
items.detail: $2,145 of low-value spend ran on frontier-tier models. Routing this work to a mid or cheap tier is the single largest savings opportunity.

items.label: Low-value spend
items.detail: Workflows tagged low value (synthetic data generation, internal documentation, SEO drafting) accounted for 35% of total spend.

items.label: Unapproved spend
items.detail: A quarter of spend, concentrated in Data Science experimentation, ran without an approval. Most of it sits in the experiment environment.

items.label: Missing owner
items.detail: $1,398 of spend carried no project owner and cannot be attributed to a budget holder.

items.text: Establish per-department monthly budgets and caps. No budget framework exists today, so every variance flag downstream depends on this first.
Route low-complexity, low-value workflows off frontier-tier models.
items.impact_note: addresses $2,145 of frontier spend on low-value work

items.text: Require an approval for any spend in the experiment environment.
Assign a project owner to every workflow; block ingestion of ownerless usage.
Review the two largest Data Science workflows for value tiering and model fit.
Data Science: is the synthetic-data generation workflow ($972, low value, mostly unapproved) still required at this volume, and can it move to a cheaper tier?
Marketing: campaign content runs on frontier models for low-complexity drafts. What is the quality reason for not using a mid tier?
Engineering: agentic test generation has a meaningful unapproved-experiment share. Should it be promoted to an approved, budgeted workflow?

needs_review.label: Sales call-note summarization, ownerless share
needs_review.reason: The ownerless portion of this workflow is below the data floor needed to attribute a cause. It is recorded but excluded from the recommendations above pending an owner mapping.

needs_review_note: Items marked "needs review" lack sufficient data to assign a cause and are excluded from the recommendations above. This is enforced in code, not left to the model's discretion.
